title: sisyrinchium idahoense var. segetum
intro: Sisyrinchium idahoense var. segetum, also known as Idaho blue-eyed grass, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Iris family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat wounds and infections. The health benefits of Idaho blue-eyed grass include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with herbal preparations typically involving the use of the plant's leaves and roots.
